title: Professional Deputies and Donees Scheme
text:
Professional Deputies and Donees Scheme - SupportGoWhere
Professional Deputies and Donees Scheme
Share link
The scheme serves individuals who may not have suitable family members or friends to appoint as their proxy decision makers. Professionals may be appointed as proxy decision makers for these individuals for remuneration.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Professional deputies have to pass a certification course and a list of stringent criteria (e.g. no history of bankruptcy or criminal proceedings related to financial dishonesty) before they can be registered
The professional deputies have to re-register every 2 years with the Office of the Public Guardian to ensure they still meet the stringent criteria
The charges of the Professional Deputies vary depending on their professions and individual case requirements. Individuals may engage them to mutually agree on the fee charge
Collapse all sections
About this support

Professionals registered under this scheme include lawyers, doctors, accountants, allied health professionals, nurses, and social workers

Professional deputies apply to Court to act for individuals who lack mental capacity (known as 'P') on property &, affairs, and personal welfare matters

Professional deputies provide a (i) P-focused plan and a (ii) Schedule of Assets as part of the application

Who is this for?

Persons who lack mental capacity (known as ‘P’) who do not have suitable family members or friends to be their proxy decision makers

Individuals with mental capacity can also choose to make a Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA), and appoint a Professional Deputy to act as a Donee for renumeration

How to apply?

Members of the public who are keen to appoint a professional deputy/donee can approach any of the individuals on this list of Registered Professional Deputies/Donees to enquire on their professional fees and to make an appointment.

Where can I find help?

View more details

MSF Consolidated Hotline: 1800-111-2222 
(To reach the Office of the Public Guardian, say "OPG" when prompted by the virtual assistant)

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Family@Enabling Village 20 Lengkok Bahru, #04-02 Singapore 159053

Scheme last updated 23 Sep 2025
